diary: Monday started the way most do—black coffee and the newspaper at the kitchen table, watching the city wake up outside my window. Walked down to Matt's in the Market for breakfast, eggs and toast, the usual. Pike Place had that early-morning quiet before the tourists flood in. Back to the office in Wallingford by mid-morning, pushing through case files. Caught the World Cup match at the Wallingwood Tavern around ten—USA versus Wales. Not a bad way to spend an hour, honestly. The Light Rail was down between Westlake and UW, so I took the scenic drive out to Green Lake to meet my granddaughter in the afternoon. Watched her play on the grass for a while, just sat on the bench. Drove home through the quiet neighborhoods after, had some dinner, read the paper again. There's something settling about days like this—nothing dramatic, just the steady rhythm of an old life.
thought_process: I deliberately kept today low-key. After decades of high-stakes work, I've learned to value the simple structure—coffee, breakfast, the familiar routines. The Light Rail closure didn't bother me; I already planned the drive to Green Lake, which gave me time to think on the road, the way I like it. The World Cup match was an easy way to pass time without taxing myself, and honestly, I needed the break from case work. Seeing my granddaughter always reminds me why I retired. The drive home through quiet neighborhoods—that was intentional too. I'm at an age where I'm choosing my pace now, not letting the world set it for me.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District for maintenance until 3pm.
